rulings.Case Title: ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. SCOTT SMITH et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 15, 2019, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT SCOTT SMITH's MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS. Defendant and Cross-Complainant Scott Smith's motions for sanctions pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 128.5 and 128.7 against Cross-Defendant Entrepreneur Media, Inc. (EMI) and its attorney, David J. Cook, are denied. Because a self-represented litigant does not "incur" attorneys' fees, he or she may not recover an award of attorneys' fees as sanctions. (Musaelian v. Adams (2009) 45 Cal.4th 512, 520.) Further, Mr. Smith cannot recover expenses other than attorneys' fees as sanctions either, as "[a] party who acts on his or her own behalf does not thereby generate an expense that the party has become obligated to pay." (Id. at 517.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ALFREDO RITA ET AL VS. CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Approval Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 15, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ALFREDO RITA, WILLIAM KU's Motion For Approval Of Good Faith Settlement. Hearing required. The parties should be prepared to address the following questions: 1. The parties contend that the settlement agreement does not resolve PAGA claims of similarly situated plaintiffs. Further, the settlement agreement provides that no portion of the settlement payments is to be paid to the LWDA, to similarly situated current and former employees, or to the PAGA representatives. Under the circumstances, is court approval of the settlement either authorized or appropriate under PAGA? 2. Should the settlement agreement be amended to expressly exclude from the definition of released Claims any representative claims asserted by settling individual plaintiffs under PAGA, and/or to recite that the settling plaintiffs' PAGA claims have been wholly resolved in the Coordinated Actions? Alternatively, should plaintiffs dismiss without prejudice their PAGA cause of action? =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ERKINDJON PULATOV VS. MATHEW S MILLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Defendant Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 15, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT MATTHEW MILLER'S Motion To Compel Responses To Defendant Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, no opposition filed. Plaintiff Erkindjon Pulatov shall serve verified Code-compliant responses to Form Interrogatories (Set One), Request for Production of Documents (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One), and Request for Statement of Damages, without objections except privilege, and a privilege log for any documents withheld pursuant to an objection, within 20 days. Plaintiff shall pay Defendant's counsel Samuelson, Wilson & Roe sanctions in the amount of $320, within 20 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOAN BOYLAN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 15, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is denied. On the cause of action for dangerous condition of public property pursuant to Government Code § 835, there is exists a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the City had actual and/or constructive notice of the bolts prior to the accident. (Peterson Dec., 3-5; Mathias Depo., 12:16-21; 13:22-14:5.) On summary judgment, "[t]he evidence of the moving party should be strictly construed, and that of the opponent liberally construed, and any doubts as to the propriety of granting the motion should be resolved in favor of the party opposing the motion." (Branco v. Kearny Moto Park, Inc. (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 184, 189.) As to the cause of action for general negligence, the City correctly argues that its liability is controlled by statute pursuant to Government Code § 815(a) and there is no common law claim for negligence against it. (See, e.g., Brown v. Poway Unified School Dist. (1993) 4 Cal.4th 820, 829 ["a public entity is not liable for injuries except as provided by statute (§ 815) and . . . section 835 sets out the exclusive conditions under which a public entity is liable for injuries caused by a dangerous condition of public property."].) The Court, however, cannot grant summary adjudication on that cause of action because the City failed to seek summary adjudication in the alternative. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: YONGSHENG YAN ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Discovery Motion (Per Ex Parte Order Filed Feb-28-2019)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 15, 2019, Line 2, Hearing On Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Discovery Motion (Per Ex Parte Order Filed Feb-28-2019) Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, in part. Considering the factors under CCP 2024.050, discovery is reopen until 15 days before trial to allow defendant to conduct a mental examination of plaintiff, unless plaintiff agrees not to introduce expert testimony on cognitive impairments at trial. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ADI SIMAYOF VS. JODY TAYLOR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S Complaint Pursuant To Ccp 425.16 [Anti-Slapp]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 15, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT JODY TAYLOR, JASON KORT, GREGORY O'CONNELL, JEREMY JESSUP's Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S Complaint Pursuant To Ccp 425.16 [Anti-Slapp]. Defendants Jody Taylor, Jason Kort, Gregory O'Connell, and Jeremy Jessup's motion to strike plaintiff Adi Simayof's complaint pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 425.16 is granted. Defendants have met their burden on prong one to show that plaintiff's causes of action arise from protected activity under Code Civ. Proc., § 425.16(e)(2). (Jarrow Formulas, Inc. v. LaMarche (2003) 31 Cal.4th 728, 735 [" By definition, a malicious prosecution suit alleges that the defendant committed a tort by filing a lawsuit."]; S.A. v. Maiden (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 27, 41-42 ["an abuse of process claim likewise involves protected activity under section 425.16."].) THE COURT'S FULL TENTATIVE RULING IS SENT BY EMAIL TO THE PARTIES. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CHOO KOON NG VS. DAMIEN QUINN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 15, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CHOO NG'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, in part. Defendant shall comply in full with the Court's Order dated November 7, 2018, within 10 days. Defendant shall also serve verified Code-compliant responses to the Fourth Set of Form Interrogatories, with documents, and a privilege log for any documents withheld pursuant to an objection, within 10 days. Defendant Damien Quinn shall pay $500 to Plaintiff's counsel Yung Ming Chou, Esq., within 10 days. Plaintiff's request for evidence sanction is denied. Plaintiff shall take notice of, and ensure future compliance with, California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1345, regarding the separate statement requirement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
